hypothesis: If catalytic surface composition is represented as two concentric shell-wise composition vectors and modeled via a chemically informed autoencoder whose latent space is used by a conditional diffusion model conditioned on adsorbate identity, target adsorption energy, and allowed elements, then the generated compositions will achieve lower Fréchet distance and leakage and higher cosine similarity than a chemically agnostic baseline, including under reduced training data and cross-dataset fine-tuning, because the PTR-based latent prior encodes elemental relationships and local structural context.
